title: centaurea irritans
intro: Centaurea irritans, also known as St. Anthony's Spur or Rough Cocklebur, is a biennial herbaceous plant native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and alleviating skin conditions like eczema and acne. Centaurea irritans can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
